checks.description: Navigate to /reports/restricted WITHOUT logging in -- verify an Access Denied / 403 response (proves anonymous access is properly blocked)
checks.evidence: Anonymous user receives HTTP 403 response code as expected. Page title indicates access was denied.

checks.description: Log in as admin via drush uli, navigate to /reports/restricted -- verify the page renders with content and HTTP 200 (proves permission-based access grants correctly)
checks.authenticated_user: admin (with test_editor role that has 'view restricted reports' permission)
checks.content_found: Yes - page displays heading 'Restricted Reports' and descriptive text 'This page is only accessible to users with the "view restricted reports" permission.'
checks.evidence: Authenticated user with proper permission receives HTTP 200 response code and page content renders correctly with expected heading and explanation text.

notes: Test setup required granting the 'view restricted reports' permission to the admin user via role assignment (test_editor role was created and added to admin user).
The administrator role itself is marked as is_admin=true, which means it doesn't need explicit permissions but inherited admin rights.
Anonymous access properly denies with HTTP 403 as expected.
Authenticated access with proper permission grant returns HTTP 200 and renders page content correctly.
